title: veronica agrestis subsp. polita
intro: Veronica agrestis subsp. polita, also known as the corn speedwell or bird's eye speedwell, is a plant species within the plantain family (Plantaginaceae) that is native to Europe and Western Asia. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which have been utilized to treat various health issues such as wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Veronica agrestis subsp. polita include reducing inflammation and fighting off infections, making it a popular herbal remedy for colds, fever, and sore throats. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used internally or applied topically to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
